compliance_documentation.regulatory_framework: 21 CFR 1002 - Reporting Requirements for Electronic Products
compliance_documentation.key_document: Form FDA 3640 - Reporting Guide for Laser Light Shows and Displays
critical_insights: CDRH does NOT approve reports or products - manufacturer certifies compliance
Accession numbers track submissions but don't indicate approval
Reports must be submitted prior to interstate commerce shipment
Incomplete reports are rejected without accession numbers

regulatory_frameworks.OSHA: General Duty Clause (29 CFR 1910) + ANSI Z136.1-2014
regulatory_frameworks.FDA: 21 CFR 1040 (FLPPS) - Federal Laser Product Performance Standards

critical_insights: No specific OSHA laser interlock regulations - enforced via General Duty Clause
Manufacturers must provide external interlocks on Class 3B/4 lasers
NO federal mandate requiring end users to actually use interlocks
Interlock connectors often shipped electrically shorted (disabled)
DIY laser construction creates safety enforcement challenges
FLPPS requirements repeated in ANSI Z136.1 Section 4.3
safety_gaps_identified: Gap between manufacturer provision and user implementation of interlocks
In-house laser construction without safety knowledge requirement
OSHA relies on ANSI/FDA standards for enforcement
